req.title: Hr-Admin deletes employee
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to delete a employee through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers DELETE /api/employees for an hr-admin role. Test design uses: EP for the happy-path (valid authenticated hr-admin deletes an existing employee); NEG for unauthenticated and forbidden-role paths; DT for the validation-error path (missing mandatory field); BVA for deleting an employee whose ID is at the numeric boundary (e.g. id=1, lowest valid PK); STC to verify the state transition from 'active employee' to 'deleted' including the audit record side-effect. Five tests are produced to satisfy the minimum of three and achieve multi-heuristic coverage.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_delete_employee_success
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as hr-admin (valid JWT)
A target employee record exists (seeded via fixture)
actions: Obtain hr-admin auth token via <fixture: hr_admin_token>
Seed a disposable employee via <helper_fn: create_employee> and capture returned id
Send DELETE /api/employees/{id} with Authorization header
Capture HTTP response status and body
Query audit log via <helper_fn: get_audit_records> for the deleted employee id
expected: Response body contains the deleted employee id
Employee is no longer retrievable (GET returns 404)
Audit log contains exactly one record for this deletion event
trace: R-WEB-003: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_hr_admin_delete_employee_success(hr_admin_token, base_url, create_employee, get_audit_records):
    employee_id = create_employee(hr_admin_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}

    resp = requests.delete(f"{base_url}/api/employees/{employee_id}", headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status_code}"

    body = resp.json()
    assert str(employee_id) in str(body), "Response body must contain deleted employee id"

    get_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/api/employees/{employee_id}", headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 404, "Deleted employee must not be retrievable"

    audit_records = get_audit_records(entity_id=employee_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_records) >= 1, "Audit record must be written on successful deletion"

tests.name: test_delete_employee_unauthenticated_returns_401
preconditions: No valid authentication token is provided
A target employee record exists (seeded via fixture)
actions: Seed a disposable employee via <helper_fn: create_employee> using <fixture: hr_admin_token> (setup only)
Send DELETE /api/employees/{id} WITHOUT an Authorization header
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_delete_employee_unauthenticated_returns_401(hr_admin_token, base_url, create_employee, get_audit_records):
    employee_id = create_employee(hr_admin_token)

    resp = requests.delete(f"{base_url}/api/employees/{employee_id}")
    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}"

    admin_headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}
    get_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/api/employees/{employee_id}", headers=admin_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200, "Employee must still exist after rejected delete"

    audit_records = get_audit_records(entity_id=employee_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_records) == 0, "No audit record should be written on unauthenticated attempt"

tests.name: test_delete_employee_forbidden_role_returns_403
preconditions: Caller is authenticated but holds role 'employee' (not hr-admin)
actions: Obtain non-admin token via <fixture: employee_token>
Seed a disposable employee via <helper_fn: create_employee> using <fixture: hr_admin_token>
Send DELETE /api/employees/{id} with the non-admin Authorization header
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_delete_employee_forbidden_role_returns_403(hr_admin_token, employee_token, base_url, create_employee, get_audit_records):
    employee_id = create_employee(hr_admin_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {employee_token}"}

    resp = requests.delete(f"{base_url}/api/employees/{employee_id}", headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"

    admin_headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}
    get_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/api/employees/{employee_id}", headers=admin_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200, "Employee must still exist after forbidden delete attempt"

    audit_records = get_audit_records(entity_id=employee_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_records) == 0, "No audit record should be written on forbidden attempt"

tests.name: test_delete_employee_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
preconditions: DELETE endpoint requires a mandatory 'reason' field in the request body
actions: Obtain hr-admin auth token via <fixture: hr_admin_token>
Seed a disposable employee via <helper_fn: create_employee>
Send DELETE /api/employees/{id} with Authorization header but omit mandatory 'reason' field in body
expected: Response body contains a validation error message referencing 'reason'
trace: R-WEB-003: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_delete_employee_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400(hr_admin_token, base_url, create_employee, get_audit_records):
    employee_id = create_employee(hr_admin_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}

    resp = requests.delete(f"{base_url}/api/employees/{employee_id}", headers=headers, json={})
    assert resp.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400, got {resp.status_code}"

    body = resp.json()
    body_str = str(body).lower()
    assert "reason" in body_str or "validation" in body_str, "Response must describe the validation failure"

    get_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/api/employees/{employee_id}", headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200, "Employee must still exist after validation-failed delete"

    audit_records = get_audit_records(entity_id=employee_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_records) == 0, "No audit record should be written on validation error"

tests.name: test_delete_employee_boundary_id_one
preconditions: An employee with the lowest valid numeric id (id=1) exists in the system or is seeded
actions: Obtain hr-admin auth token via <fixture: hr_admin_token>
Ensure employee with id=1 exists via <helper_fn: ensure_employee_exists>(id=1)
Send DELETE /api/employees/1 with Authorization header and mandatory reason field
Query audit log for entity id=1
expected: Employee with id=1 is no longer retrievable (GET returns 404)
Audit record exists for id=1 DELETE action
trace: R-WEB-003: boundary employee id at minimum valid value (1)
R-WEB-003: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BOUNDARY_ID = 1

def test_delete_employee_boundary_id_one(hr_admin_token, base_url, ensure_employee_exists, get_audit_records):
    ensure_employee_exists(BOUNDARY_ID, hr_admin_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {"reason": "BVA boundary deletion test"}

    resp = requests.delete(f"{base_url}/api/employees/{BOUNDARY_ID}", headers=headers, json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status_code}"

    body = resp.json()
    assert str(BOUNDARY_ID) in str(body), "Response must contain the boundary employee id"

    get_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/api/employees/{BOUNDARY_ID}", headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 404, "Boundary employee must not be retrievable after deletion"

    audit_records = get_audit_records(entity_id=BOUNDARY_ID, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_records) >= 1, "Audit record must exist for boundary id deletion"

tests.name: test_delete_employee_state_transition_active_to_deleted
preconditions: Employee lifecycle states include: ACTIVE -> DELETED
actions: Obtain hr-admin token via <fixture: hr_admin_token>
Seed employee in ACTIVE state via <helper_fn: create_employee>; verify state is ACTIVE
Send DELETE /api/employees/{id} with Authorization header and mandatory reason field
Retrieve employee record (expect 404 or status=DELETED depending on API contract)
Verify audit log records the ACTIVE->DELETED transition
expected: Pre-delete GET returns employee with status ACTIVE
Post-delete GET returns 404 or status field equals DELETED
Audit log records state transition from ACTIVE to DELETED
trace: R-WEB-003: employee is deleted (state ACTIVE -> DELETED)
R-WEB-003: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_delete_employee_state_transition_active_to_deleted(hr_admin_token, base_url, create_employee, get_audit_records):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    employee_id = create_employee(hr_admin_token)

    pre = requests.get(f"{base_url}/api/employees/{employee_id}", headers=headers)
    assert pre.status_code == 200
    pre_body = pre.json()
    assert pre_body.get("status", "ACTIVE").upper() == "ACTIVE", "Employee must be ACTIVE before deletion"

    payload = {"reason": "STC state transition test"}
    del_resp = requests.delete(f"{base_url}/api/employees/{employee_id}", headers=headers, json=payload)
    assert del_resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {del_resp.status_code}"

    post = requests.get(f"{base_url}/api/employees/{employee_id}", headers=headers)
    if post.status_code == 200:
        assert post.json().get("status", "").upper() == "DELETED", "Status must transition to DELETED"
    else:
        assert post.status_code == 404, "Deleted employee must return 404 or DELETED status"

    audit_records = get_audit_records(entity_id=employee_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_records) >= 1, "Audit must record ACTIVE->DELETED transition"
